tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text:
{"number":57006,"title":"[Mgmt Generator] DefaultApiVersion crashes on multi-service client (Compute migration blocked)","body":"## Bug Description

`ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion` (line 147) calls `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last()` which throws `Sequence contains no elements` for multi-service clients.

`csharp
// ManagementInputLibrary.cs:147
internal string DefaultApiVersion => InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last();
`

## Root Cause

The Compute TypeSpec spec uses a combined client pattern:

`	sp
@client({
  name: "ComputeClient",
  service: [Compute, ComputeDisk, ComputeGallery, ComputeSku],
})
namespace ComputeCombine;
`

This produces a code model where:
- **Top-level `ApiVersions` is empty** (the combined namespace has no versions)
- **Each child service has its own API version** embedded in operation parameters:
  - `Compute`: `2025-04-01`
  - `ComputeDisk`: `2025-01-02`
  - `ComputeGallery`: `2025-03-03`
  - `ComputeSku`: `2021-07-01`
- Otherwise the code model is complete: 51 child clients, 147 models, 159 enums, all operations properly mapped.

## Design Issue

Having a **global** `DefaultApiVersion` is architecturally wrong for multi-service clients. Looking at existing generated code (e.g. KeyVault), the API version only flows to REST client constructors as a fallback:

`csharp
// KeyVaultCollection.cs (generated)
_vaultsRestClient = new Vaults(_vaultsClientDiagnostics, Pipeline, Endpoint, 
                               keyVaultApiVersion ?? "2025-05-01");
`

Each REST client already stores its own `_apiVersion` field. For multi-service clients like Compute, each REST client should receive its **service-specific** API version (derived from the operation's `apiVersion` parameter default), not a single global default.

## Callers of DefaultApiVersion

Only 3 places in the generator use it:
1. `MockableResourceProvider.cs:116` — passes literal to REST client constructor
2. `ResourceClientProvider.cs:298` — `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))`
3. `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs:291` — same pattern

All 3 should derive the API version from the specific REST client's operations rather than a global value.

## Impact

This blocks the `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` TypeSpec migration. The code model is otherwise complete and ready for generation.

## Reproduction

1. Set up `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `specification/compute/resource-manager/Microsoft.Compute/Compute`
2. Add `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter to the spec's `tspconfig.yaml`
3. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`
4. Generator crashes with: `Sequence contains no elements` at `ManagementInputLibrary.get_DefaultApiVersion()`","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57006","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"profile_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4"},"author_association":"MEMBER","labels":["Mgmt"],"reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"created_at":"2026-03-12T07:57:02Z","updated_at":"2026-03-12T07:57:02Z"}

3. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`
4. arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This is a confirmed bug in the management plane SDK generator (`Azure.Generator.Management`) where `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion` calls `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last()`, which throws `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` for multi-service combined clients (like Compute). The root cause is a flawed assumption that a single global API version exists for all clients, which doesn't hold for specs using the `@client({ service: [...] })` combined-client pattern.

**Reporter:** Repository member `@ArcturusZhang` — not customer-reported.
**Blocking:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` TypeSpec migration.
**Affected component:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/ManagementInputLibrary.cs` (line 147) and its 3 callers.

<details>
<summary>📋 Root Cause Analysis</summary>

The bug is in `ManagementInputLibrary.cs`:

```csharp
// Line 147 — crashes when ApiVersions is empty (multi-service combined clients)
internal string DefaultApiVersion => InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last();
```

The Compute TypeSpec spec uses a combined-client pattern:

```typespec
@client({
  name: "ComputeClient",
  service: [Compute, ComputeDisk, ComputeGallery, ComputeSku],
})
namespace ComputeCombine;
```

In this pattern:
- The top-level `InputNamespace.ApiVersions` is **empty** (the aggregated namespace has no direct API versions).
- API versions live **per sub-service**, surfaced through operation parameter defaults:
  - `Compute` → `2025-04-01`
  - `ComputeDisk` → `2025-01-02`
  - `ComputeGallery` → `2025-03-03`
  - `ComputeSku` → `2021-07-01`

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔧 Suggested Fix Approach</summary>

The `DefaultApiVersion` property needs to be removed or made per-REST-client. There are 3 call sites to update:

| File | Line | Current Usage |
|------|------|---------------|
| `MockableResourceProvider.cs` | 116 | Passes as literal to REST client ctor |
| `ResourceClientProvider.cs` | 298 | `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))` |
| `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs` | 291 | Same pattern |

**Recommended fix:** Each of these 3 providers already knows which REST client it's generating for. They should derive the API version from the REST client's own operations (look up the `apiVersion` constant/enum parameter default on any operation belonging to that client), rather than using a global value.

A safe fallback chain:
1. Try to get API version from the specific `InputClient`'s operations' `apiVersion` parameter default
2. Fall back to `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.LastOrDefault()` for single-service specs (backward compat)
3. If still null, throw a descriptive exception

```csharp
// Example helper
private static string? GetApiVersionForClient(InputClient client)
{
    return client.Operations
        .SelectMany(op => op.Parameters)
        .FirstOrDefault(p => p.Name == "apiVersion" && p.DefaultValue != null)
        ?.DefaultValue?.Value as string;
}
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🧪 Reproduction Steps</summary>

1. Clone the Compute TypeSpec spec from `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Set up `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `specification/compute/resource-manager/Microsoft.Compute/Compute`
3. Add `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter to the spec's `tspconfig.yaml`
4. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`
5. Generator crashes: `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` at `ManagementInputLibrary.get_DefaultApiVersion()`

**Quick unit test repro:** Create an `InputNamespace` with empty `ApiVersions` and multiple child `InputClient` instances with `apiVersion` parameters, then invoke `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ Sub-task Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Remove/refactor `DefaultApiVersion` in `ManagementInputLibrary.cs` to avoid crashing on empty `ApiVersions`
- [ ] Update `MockableResourceProvider.cs:116` to derive API version from the specific REST client's operations
- [ ] Update `ResourceClientProvider.cs:298` similarly
- [ ] Update `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs:291` similarly
- [ ] Add a unit/integration test covering the multi-service combined-client scenario (empty top-level `ApiVersions`)
- [ ] Validate Compute TypeSpec generation succeeds end-to-end after the fix
- [ ] Run `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to regenerate test projects and ensure no regressions

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [ManagementInputLibrary.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/ManagementInputLibrary.cs) — line 147
- [MockableResourceProvider.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/MockableResourceProvider.cs) — line 116
- [TypeSpec `@client` decorator docs](https://typespec.io/docs/libraries/http/reference/decorators#@client) — combined client pattern
- [http-client-csharp-mgmt generator README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)

</details>

**Owners (per CODEOWNERS):** @JoshLove-msft @m-nash @jorgerangel-msft @live1206 @ArcturusZhang

The root cause is a flawed assumption that a single global API version exists for all clients, which doesn't hold for specs using the `@client({ service: [...] })` combined-client pattern.

**Reporter:** Repository member — not customer-reported.

<details>
<summary>📋 Root Cause Analysis</summary>

```csharp

```

```typespec
@client({
  name: "ComputeClient",
  service: [Compute, ComputeDisk, ComputeGallery, ComputeSku],

namespace ComputeCombine;
```

- The top-level `InputNamespace.ApiVersions` is **empty** (the aggregated namespace has no direct API versions).

  - `Compute` → `2025-04-01`, `ComputeDisk` → `2025-01-02`, `ComputeGallery` → `2025-03-03`, `ComputeSku` → `2021-07-01`

| File | Line | Current Usage |
|------|------|---------------|
| `MockableResourceProvider.cs` | 116 | Passes as literal to REST client ctor |
| `ResourceClientProvider.cs` | 298 | `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))` |
| `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs` | 291 | Same pattern |

**Recommended fix:** Each of these 3 providers already knows which REST client it's generating for. They should derive the API version from the REST client's own operations (look up the `apiVersion` constant/enum parameter default on any operation belonging to that client), rather than using a global value.

1. Try to get API version from the specific `InputClient`'s operations' `apiVersion` parameter default

```csharp
// Example helper
private static string? GetApiVersionForClient(InputClient client)
{

        .SelectMany(op => op.Parameters)

```

</details>

<details>

1. Clone the Compute TypeSpec spec from `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Set up `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `specification/compute/resource-manager/Microsoft.Compute/Compute`
3. Add `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter to the spec's `tspconfig.yaml`
4. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`
5. Generator crashes: `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` at `ManagementInputLibrary.get_DefaultApiVersion()`

**Quick unit test repro:** Create an `InputNamespace` with empty `ApiVersions` and multiple child `InputClient` instances with `apiVersion` parameters, then invoke `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ Sub-task Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Remove/refactor `DefaultApiVersion` in `ManagementInputLibrary.cs` to avoid crashing on empty `ApiVersions`
- [ ] Update `MockableResourceProvider.cs:116` to derive API version from the specific REST client's operations
- [ ] Update `ResourceClientProvider.cs:298` similarly
- [ ] Update `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs:291` similarly
- [ ] Add a unit/integration test covering the multi-service combined-client scenario (empty top-level `ApiVersions`)
- [ ] Validate Compute TypeSpec generation succeeds end-to-end after the fix
- [ ] Run `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to regenerate test projects and ensure no regressions

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [ManagementInputLibrary.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/ManagementInputLibrary.cs) — line 147
- [MockableResourceProvider.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/MockableResourceProvider.cs) — line 116
- [TypeSpec `@client` decorator docs](https://typespec.io/docs/libraries/http/reference/decorators#@client) — combined client pattern
- [http-client-csharp-mgmt generator README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)
